FoSTaC Training — Now Mandatory

Photo Identity Proof

Proof of Possession of Premises

An electricity bill or rent agreement in your name at the business address. A photo of the original works. Operating from shared space? A No-Objection Certificate from the owner is also accepted.

03
Water Analysis Report
NABL-accredited lab · PDF · Not older than 6 months
✓ Lab report approved
▾

Required because you use water as a food ingredient. Must be from a NABL-accredited lab, issued within the last 6 months. Book a test through One Stop Shop — the lab uploads the report directly to your profile.

One Stop Shop 🛒

Book services, safety kits, and hygiene checks — all from one place

💡You need a water potability report. Book below — the lab uploads directly to your profile. You don't need to do anything else.
Shillong Analytical Lab
📍 1.2 km · ⭐ 4.8 · Next: Tomorrow 9am
Meghalaya Enviro Lab
📍 2.4 km · ⭐ 4.6 · Next: Today 4pm
North East Testing Services
📍 3.1 km · ⭐ 4.7 · Next: Tomorrow 2pm
About Water Testing
A water analysis report from a NABL-accredited laboratory is mandatory if water is used as a food ingredient in your business.

The lab will test your water source for microbial and chemical parameters as per FSSAI standards. Results are typically available within 48–72 hours and uploaded directly to your application.

Cost: ₹300–₹800 depending on the lab and test package.
🩺A Medical Fitness Certificate is required for each food handler employed in your business. Book a health check-up at a registered clinic below.
Dr. Lyngdoh Wellness Clinic
📍 0.8 km · ⭐ 4.9 · Walk-in available
Civil Hospital Shillong
📍 1.5 km · ⭐ 4.5 · Mon–Sat 9am–5pm
What's Checked
The medical examination covers:

✅ Communicable disease screening
✅ Skin condition check
✅ Typhoid & hepatitis screening
✅ General fitness assessment

Cost: ₹150–₹400 per person. Certificate valid for 1 year and must be renewed annually.
🔥A Fire Safety NOC from your local fire department is required for all food establishments with open-flame cooking. Schedule an inspection below.
Shillong Fire & Emergency Services
📍 Government body · Official NOC issuer
🧯 Required Equipment
✅ 2 kg ABC fire extinguisher
✅ Clearly marked emergency exit
✅ Fire blanket near cooking area
✅ No blocked fire escapes
About Fire Safety NOC
The Fire NOC certifies that your premises comply with fire safety norms. Required for:

🔥 Restaurants & cafés with gas cooking
🔥 Cloud kitchens and dark kitchens
🔥 Canteens with industrial equipment

Validity: 1–3 years depending on establishment type. Fees vary by municipality. Cost typically ₹1,000–₹5,000.

Charged alongside your license payment

🔬 11 Tests Across 6 Modules
Module 1 — Dairy & Liquids
Lactometer test (water dilution) · Iodine test (starch in milk/paneer)
Module 2 — Water Quality
pH · Chlorine sanitisation · Hardness (test strips)
Module 3 — Grains & Rice
Chuna test (turmeric in rice) · Visual inspection for Dhatura seeds
Module 4 — Sugar & Dry Spices
HCl test (chalk in sugar) · Magnet test (iron filings in spices/tea)
Module 5 — Fresh Produce
Oil rub test (malachite green / rhodamine B on vegetables)
Module 6 — Edible Oils
TPM strip (frying oil quality) · Sensory check (rancidity/kerosene) · TOCP test (toxic oil)
Why test your own food?
Regular self-testing helps you:

✅ Catch adulteration before it reaches customers
✅ Pass surprise FSSAI inspections confidently
✅ Protect your business from liability
✅ Build a verifiable hygiene record

No lab visit needed — all tests can be done at your stall or kitchen in under 10 minutes each.
📖Step-by-step testing procedures for all 11 tests in your kit. Click any module to expand the full instructions.
🥛 Module 1 — Dairy & Liquids
1
Lactometer Test — Milk Density & Water Dilution
Equipment needed
Lactometer · Tall measuring cylinder · Milk sample
Procedure
Fill the tall cylinder with the milk sample. Gently lower the lactometer into the milk and allow it to float freely without touching the sides. Wait for it to settle and read the value at the surface of the milk.
⚠️
Detection: A reading below the standard range (28–30) indicates water dilution. Pure full-fat milk typically reads 26–32 on the lactometer scale.
2
Iodine Test — Starch in Milk / Paneer
Equipment needed
Iodine solution 1% · Dropper · Beaker · Milk or paneer sample
Procedure
Add 2–3ml of the milk sample (or dissolve a small piece of paneer in water) to a beaker. Using the dropper, add 2–3 drops of the 1% iodine solution directly to the sample.
🔵
Detection: Formation of a blue or blue-black colour indicates the presence of starch — a common adulterant used to thicken milk or increase paneer yield. No colour change = pure.
💧 Module 2 — Water Quality & Hygiene
3
pH, Chlorine & Hardness — Water Test Strips
Equipment needed
Multi-parameter water test strips · Fresh water sample or vegetable wash water
Procedure
Dip the test strip into a fresh water sample for exactly 2 seconds. Remove and hold horizontally. Wait 30–60 seconds then compare the colour pads on the strip to the chart provided in the packaging.
pH: Ideal range is 6.5–8.5. Too acidic or alkaline can affect food taste and safety.
Chlorine: For vegetable wash water, confirms sanitisation solution is at 50–100 ppm to kill pathogens. Tap water should show trace chlorine.
Hardness: Very hard water causes scale buildup in kitchen equipment and kettles. Ideal below 200 mg/L.
🍚 Module 3 — Grains & Rice
4
Chuna (Lime) Test — Turmeric in Sella Rice
Equipment needed
Soaked lime (chuna) · Glass plate or petri dish · Rice sample
Procedure
Take a teaspoon of rice on a glass plate. Sprinkle a small amount of soaked lime (chuna) directly onto the rice grains and observe the colour change.
🟥
Detection: Pure grains will show no colour change. If the grains turn red or pink, turmeric or artificial colour has been added — a common adulterant in sella (parboiled) rice.
5
Visual Grain Inspection — Dhatura Seeds & Impurities
Equipment needed
Glass plate or petri dish · Good lighting · Sample of food grains
Procedure
Spread a small quantity of food grains in a single layer on the glass plate. Examine carefully under good light using your eyes or a magnifying glass.
⚠️
Detection: Look for Dhatura seeds (flat with edges, blackish-brown — toxic if consumed), rodent hair, insect excreta, or stones. Any foreign matter indicates contamination.
🌶️ Module 4 — Sugar & Dry Spices
6
HCl Test — Chalk in Sugar
Equipment needed
Dilute Hydrochloric Acid (HCl) · Beaker · Dropper · 10g sugar sample
Procedure
Dissolve 10g of sugar in a beaker with a small amount of water. Once dissolved, add a few drops of dilute HCl solution using the dropper and observe immediately.
🫧
Detection: Effervescence (bubbling) indicates the presence of chalk powder or carbonates — used to add weight to sugar. No fizzing = pure. Alternatively, dissolve sugar in plain water — chalk settles at the bottom.
7
Magnet Test — Iron Filings in Spices & Tea
Equipment needed
Neodymium magnet · Glass plate · Coriander powder, tea leaves, or any dry spice
Procedure
Spread the tea leaves or spice powder in a thin layer on a glass plate. Slowly move the neodymium magnet through and just above the sample in a sweeping motion. Observe the magnet closely.
🧲
Detection: Iron filings will visibly cling to the magnet if the sample is adulterated. Iron powder is added to increase the weight of spices and tea — it is a health hazard if consumed in large quantities.
🥬 Module 5 — Fresh Produce
8
Paraffin / Oil Rub Test — Artificial Dyes on Vegetables
Equipment needed
Paraffin liquid or vegetable oil · Cotton balls · Chilli, bitter gourd, sweet potato, or leafy vegetables
Procedure
Soak a cotton ball in paraffin liquid or vegetable oil. Firmly rub the outer surface of the vegetable — focus on the green skin of chillies or bitter gourd, or the red skin of sweet potatoes. Observe the colour absorbed by the cotton ball.
🎨
Detection: If the cotton ball absorbs bright green or red colour, artificial dyes (Malachite Green or Rhodamine B) have been applied. These are banned carcinogenic dyes. No colour transfer from natural vegetables is normal.
🫒 Module 6 — Edible Oils & Frying Fats
9
TPM Strip Test — Frying Oil Quality
Equipment needed
TPM test strip · Hot frying oil (within manufacturer's temperature range)
Procedure
Dip the TPM test strip into the hot frying oil. Ensure the oil is within the correct temperature range specified on the strip packaging. Hold for the indicated time, then compare the colour to the reference chart.
🚫
Detection: A reading above 25% TPM (Total Polar Materials) means the oil has degraded beyond safe use and must be discarded. Reusing degraded oil generates harmful compounds and is a health risk.
10
Sensory Evaluation — Rancidity & Mineral Oil Contamination
Procedure
Pour a small sample of oil into a clean beaker. First observe its clarity and colour against a light source, then smell the sample carefully.
Contamination: A kerosene-like or petroleum smell indicates the oil may be contaminated with mineral oil — a banned adulterant.
Rancidity: A bitter taste or stale "off" smell indicates oxidation — the oil should be discarded.
Purity: Pure winterised salad oils should appear brilliant, clear, and limpid (no cloudiness or sediment).
11
TOCP Test — Tri-Ortho-Cresyl-Phosphate Detection (Toxic Oil)
Equipment needed
Beaker · Yellow solid butter (Amul or local) · Oil sample
Procedure
Take 2ml of the oil sample in a clean beaker. Add a small amount of solid yellow butter and stir gently. Observe the colour of the mixture immediately.
🔴
Detection: Immediate formation of a red colour indicates the presence of TOCP (Tri-Ortho-Cresyl-Phosphate) — a highly toxic organophosphate compound that causes paralysis and is sometimes used to adulterate edible oils. Discard the oil immediately and report to FSSAI.

Why subscribe?
Regular hygiene audits help you maintain compliance and build customer trust. Benefits include:

✅ Avoid surprise FSSAI inspections
✅ Catch issues before they become violations
✅ Build a hygiene track record for license upgrades
✅ Display a verified Hygiene Badge on your stall
✅ Priority support if you face a compliance query

Subscriptions can be cancelled any time with 7 days notice. No refund for partial months.

⚡ Temporary License Fast-Track

Upload your 3 KYC documents
and start operating today

No water report, no inspection — just 3 basic documents. You'll get a Temporary License immediately, then have 2 months to complete full compliance.

🏪
What you can do with a Temporary License
Operate a small kitchen (max 500 sq.ft) · Use a gas stove · Sell snacks & packaged goods · Start learning FSSAI safety standards
01
Photo Identity Proof
Aadhaar · PAN · Driving Licence · Voter ID · Passport
Any valid government-issued photo ID. A clear phone photo is fine — no scanning needed. Ensure all four corners are visible.
✅ Aadhaar (most common) ✅ PAN Card ✅ Driving Licence
02
Address Proof — Geotagged Photos
2 photos of your restaurant/stall with GPS location enabled
Take 2 photos of your food stall or kitchen with your phone's location turned ON. The GPS coordinates will be auto-embedded in the photo.
🥔 Tip: One photo of the storefront, one of the cooking area. Daylight photos work best.
03
PAN / Tax Proof
PAN Card or latest ITR · Used for turnover verification
A clear photo or PDF of your PAN card. If you don't have PAN, your latest Income Tax Return (ITR) acknowledgement is also accepted.
🥔 Don't have PAN? No problem — just upload your latest electricity bill or GST registration as an alternative.

✓
Under ₹12 Lakh / year
Qualifies for Temporary / Basic Registration
✓

✓

🎉

You Qualify!

Great news — you are eligible for a Temporary Food License. It will be issued instantly after you complete the next steps.

What you get:
✅ Instant license issuance
✅ 30-day stall license or 60-day business license
✅ Operate legally from Day 1
✅ Upgrade path to permanent license (if needed)
📋

You Need a Different License

Based on your answers, a Temporary License may not be the right fit. We'll guide you to the correct category.

What is this license for? 📋

Help us understand your purpose so we can issue the right license with the right validity.

✓
🛖
Stall / Event (1 Month)
Setting up a stall for a festival, event, or one-time occasion. Valid for 30 days — no follow-up conversion required.
30-day license No conversion needed
✓
🏗️
Business Setup (60 Days)
Starting a permanent food business. Receive a 60-day temporary license while you complete full compliance and get a permanent license.
60-day license Convert to permanent

📍 Step 4 of 8 — Photos

Photos of your setup 📸

Upload geotagged photos of your setup and equipment. Turn on GPS before clicking photos.

📍
GPS must be ON
Enable location on your phone before taking photos. The coordinates get embedded automatically.
1
📸 Setup / Stall Photo
Geotagged photo of your stall or kitchen entrance
Take a wide shot showing your full setup — cart, stall, or kitchen. Ensure the location PIN shows in your camera app.
2
🔧 Equipment / Cooking Area Photo
Geotagged photo of your stove, utensils, and prep area
Show your cooking equipment clearly — gas stove, cooking pots, storage containers. Daylight photos work best.
Upload Progress 0 / 2
📄 Step 5 of 8 — KYC

Basic KYC documents 📄

Just 2 documents needed. Phone photos are fine — no scanning required.

🥔
Potato Tip
Aadhaar + cart purchase bill (or rent agreement) is all you need. No notarization, no office visits.
1
🪪 Photo Identity Proof
Aadhaar · PAN · Voter ID · Driving Licence · Passport
Any government-issued photo ID. Clear photo of both sides. Up to 5MB. JPG, PNG or PDF.
✅ AI Scan: Identity verified
2
🏪 Proof of Establishment
Cart / stall purchase bill OR rent agreement
Upload one of the following:
🛺 Cart/Stall Purchase Bill 📋 Rent Agreement 💡 Electricity Bill
Upload Progress 0 / 2
📜 Step 6 of 8 — T&C

Operating Rules & Terms 📜

Please read and accept the conditions of your Temporary License before it is issued.

⚠️ Restricted Operating Rules
📐
Maximum Area: 500 sq. ft.
Your kitchen or stall must not exceed 500 sq. ft. of food preparation area.
🔥
Gas Stove Only — No Industrial Equipment
Commercial deep fryers, blast chillers, or industrial cooking units are not permitted.
🥙
Low-Risk Foods Only
You may not sell raw meat, unpasteurised dairy, or products requiring cold chain certification.
⏰
Valid for 60 Days Only
You must complete compliance requirements and upgrade within 60 days or cease operations.
I confirm that:

🚀 Step 8 of 8 — Next Steps

Complete your compliance 🗺️

Three steps to secure your permanent license. Work through them at your own pace.

⏰
60 days remaining on your Temporary License
Complete all three steps before your license expires to upgrade automatically.
💧
Water Analysis Report
A water potability test from a NABL-accredited laboratory is required. The lab uploads the report directly to your profile.
Shillong Analytical Lab
📍 1.2 km · ⭐ 4.8 · Next: Tomorrow 9am
Meghalaya Enviro Lab
📍 2.4 km · ⭐ 4.6 · Next: Today 4pm

✅

FoSTaC Food Safety Training
Mandatory for all food businesses. Certificate issued on completion. Choose online or offline.

Self-paced · 4 hrs · Hindi & English
₹200 · Enrol Now →

₹350 · Find Centre →
